title: Brim (zui)
overview: | Purpose | Query | | --- | --- | | Search a value (IP, string) | 10.0.0.1 | | Filter by log type | path == "conn" | | Filter by a specific field | id.origh == 192.168.121.40 | | Logical operators | 192 and NTP |

study.overview: | Purpose | Query | | --- | --- | | Search a value (IP, string) | 10.0.0.1 | | Filter by log type | path == "conn" | | Filter by a specific field | id.origh == 192.168.121.40 | | Logical operators | 192 and NTP |
study.whySoc: Use this note as an analyst reference: understand the concept, identify the relevant telemetry, pivot through evidence, and document the decision path clearly.
workflow: Identify the user, host, process, network flow, or identity object involved.
Collect the highest-value logs first and preserve timestamps and assumptions.
Pivot through related notes, telemetry sources, and MITRE references.
Document findings, gaps, containment options, and detection improvements.

splunkExamples.code:
_path == "http" and id.resp_p == 80       # HTTP traffic (port 80)
_path == "http" and method == "POST"     # POST requests
| where host contains "malicious-domain.com"
| where uri contains "../" or uri contains "UNION"
| where body contains "password"

_path == "dns"
| where query contains ".onion" or query contains "suspiciousdomain"
| where query == "example.com"
| where dns.resp.ip == "<suspected_ip>"

_path == "kerberos"
| where msg_type in {10, 12}               # TGT/TGS requests
| where error_code exists                  # Failed auths

importantCommands.code:
# Absolute window
timestamp >= 2025-07-01T00:00:00Z and timestamp < 2025-07-07T00:00:00Z

# Last hour
timestamp >= now() - 1h

# Group into 15m buckets
| bucket span=15m timestamp
| count() by _path
